name: Premium Everest Base Camp Trek
description: A 14-day premium trekking experience to Mount Everest Base Camp featuring trusted local guides, carefully paced acclimatization, and high-quality tea house accommodation.
longDescription: The Everest Base Camp trek is more than just a trek – it's a pilgrimage to the core of the Himalayas. Rising to an altitude of 5,364m, this journey takes you through high-altitude landscapes, Sherpa villages, and ancient monasteries. Experience the unique culture of the Khumbu region while standing in the shadow of Mount Everest.
highlights: Stand at the foot of Mount Everest (8,848m)
Iconic flight from Kathmandu to Lukla
Explore the vibrant Sherpa capital, Namche Bazaar

itinerary.title: Fly from Kathmandu to Lukla and trek to Phakding
itinerary.experienceNote: The flight to Lukla is a thrilling start. The trail to Phakding is relatively flat, mostly following the Dudh Koshi river, making it a great day to ease into the altitude.

itinerary.title: Trek from Phakding to Namche Bazaar
itinerary.experienceNote: A challenging ascent into the Sagarmatha National Park. You will cross the famous Hillary Suspension Bridge and catch your first glimpse of Mount Everest.

itinerary.title: Acclimatization Day at Namche Bazaar
itinerary.experienceNote: Climb high, sleep low. We hike up to the Everest View Hotel (3,880m) to jumpstart acclimatization before returning to Namche for the night.

itinerary.title: Trek from Namche Bazaar to Tengboche
itinerary.experienceNote: Trail passes beautiful rhododendron forests. Tengboche is home to the largest monastery in the Khumbu region, offering phenomenal views of Ama Dablam.

itinerary.title: Trek from Tengboche to Dingboche
itinerary.experienceNote: We cross the tree line today. The air becomes noticeably thinner as we follow the Imja Khola river up into the higher alpine zone.

itinerary.title: Acclimatization Day at Dingboche
itinerary.experienceNote: Crucial second acclimatization day. We hike up Nangkartshang Peak (~5,000m) for panoramic views of Makalu and Lhotse to condition the body.

itinerary.title: Trek from Dingboche to Lobuche
itinerary.experienceNote: We pass the Thukla Pass memorials (dedications to climbers who lost their lives on Everest). A somber and breathtakingly beautiful section.

itinerary.title: Trek to Everest Base Camp, sleep at Gorakshep
itinerary.experienceNote: The climax of the trek. We navigate the rocky Khumbu glacier moraine to reach EBC, celebrate the achievement, then descend to Gorakshep for sleep.

itinerary.title: Hike Kala Patthar, trek to Pheriche
itinerary.experienceNote: Early morning push up Kala Patthar for the absolute best, unobstructed sunrise view of Mount Everest. We then begin the rapid descent to thicker air.

itinerary.title: Trek from Pheriche to Namche Bazaar
itinerary.experienceNote: A long but heavily descending day. Breathing becomes dramatically easier. You can finally enjoy a hot shower and bakery treats in Namche.

itinerary.title: Trek from Namche Bazaar to Lukla
itinerary.experienceNote: The final trekking day. We re-cross the suspension bridges, exit the National Park, and celebrate our last night with the sherpa crew in Lukla.

itinerary.title: Fly from Lukla to Kathmandu
itinerary.experienceNote: Early morning picturesque flight back to the capital. Delays due to weather are common, so we keep buffer days.

included: Khumbu Pasang Lhamu Rural Municipality entry permit
Porter service (1 porter for 2 trekkers, max 15kg each)
Premium tea house accommodations during the trek
Three meals a day (Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner) on the trek
Comprehensive medical kit carried by the guide
excluded: Mandatory remote medical evacuation travel insurance
Hot showers, Wi-Fi, and battery charging at tea houses

estimateBreakdown.description: Sagarmatha National Park & Khumbu Local Municipality Entry Fees
Round trip Kathmandu to Lukla flights (foreigner rate)
Licensed Guide and Shared Porter for 12 days
Accommodation and 3 meals/day in tea houses
Tax, office service charge, emergency coordination setup

weatherGuide.condition: Clear but extremely cold. Heavy snow can block passes.
Spring season. Excellent visibility. Blooming rhododendrons.
Monsoon summer. Muddy trails, leeches, obscured mountain views.
Autumn season. Crystal clear skies, mild temperatures, highly stable.
Early winter. Clear views but severe night-time drops.

mustHave: Water purification tablets or UV filter

helicopterEvacuation.details: Helicopter evacuation requires explicit authorization from your insurance provider or a cash deposit. We manage ground-to-air communication. Your insurance MUST cover 'High Altitude Trekking up to 6000m' and specifically include Helicopter Evacuation. Do not buy standard travel insurance.

symptoms: Persistent headache not cured by painkillers
Loss of appetite and severe nausea
prevention: Heed the 'Climb High, Sleep Low' rule on acclimatization days
Avoid alcohol and heavy meat consumption at high altitudes
altitudeSicknessGuide.policy: Our guides carry oximeters to check your oxygen saturation twice daily. If symptoms progress beyond a mild headache to AMS, the absolute protocol is immediate descent. Safety supersedes the itinerary.

faqs.question: How hard is the Everest Base Camp Trek?
faqs.answer: The Everest Base Camp trek is moderately difficult. It requires no technical climbing skills or mountaineering equipment, just walking. However, the high altitude makes cardiovascular exertion feel much harder. You should be able to comfortably hike 10km a day with a small backpack to succeed.

faqs.question: Do I need to train before trekking to Everest Base Camp?
faqs.answer: Yes, cardiovascular training is essential. We recommend starting 8-12 weeks prior. Focus on stair climbing, running, or hiking 2-3 times a week with a weighted 5kg backpack to condition your leg muscles and lungs.

faqs.question: What happens if I get Altitude Sickness (AMS)?
faqs.answer: If you experience mild symptoms (headache), our guides will have you rest and measure oxygen levels. If symptoms escalate to nausea, dizziness, or fluid in the lungs (HAPE), the strict protocol is immediate descent, often facilitated by a helicopter evacuation if walking is impossible.

faqs.question: Is drinking water safe along the Everest trail?
faqs.answer: Tap and river water are not safe to drink raw. You must bring water purification tablets (Aquatabs), a UV purifier (Steripen), or a filter bottle (Lifestraw). Buying bottled plastic water is discouraged and banned in parts of the Khumbu region for environmental reasons.

reviews.author: Sarah Jenkins
reviews.content: An impeccably managed trek. Our guide Phurba ensured our pacing was perfect. I was terrified of altitude sickness, but their 'climb high, sleep low' enforcement kept me perfectly healthy all the way to EBC.

reviews.author: Marcus Thorne
reviews.content: Transparency from the start. The cost breakdown was exactly as described, no hidden fees. Be sure to rent the -20 sleeping bag in Kathmandu like they suggest, Gorakshep is freezing.

expertGuides.name: Pasang Sherpa
expertGuides.bio: Born in the Khumbu valley, Pasang has summited Everest twice and now leads premier trekking groups. He is an expert in dynamic acclimatization strategies.
